---
title: "FinalProj"
author: "Adam Walters"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```


```{r}
library(tidyverse)
nba_data <- read.csv("25-25PlayerStats.csv")

# Filter for MpG >= 4 and GP >= 3
cleaned_data <- nba_data %>%
  filter(MpG >= 4, GP >= 3, ORtg <= 160, ORtg >= 50,TO. >= 2,TO. <=50) %>%
  # Ensure stats are numeric for analysis
  mutate(across(c(USG., TS., TO., ORtg, eFG.), as.numeric)) %>%
  drop_na(USG., TS., TO., ORtg) # Remove any remaining incomplete rows

# Quick look at the data
summary(cleaned_data)
```


## Multiple Linear Regression
Implements linear regression model $ORtg = \beta_0 + \beta_1(USG\%) + \beta_2(TS\%) + \beta_3(TO\%) + \epsilon$
(revisit if we are instructed to use different method for multiple linear regression)


Hypothesis Testing: Permutation test
```{r}
# Define groups 
high_usage <- cleaned_data$TS.[cleaned_data$USG. > 25]
low_usage <- cleaned_data$TS.[cleaned_data$USG. <= 25]

# Observed difference
obs_diff <- mean(high_usage) - mean(low_usage)

# Permutation Test 
set.seed(4714)

n_permutations <- 10000
perm_diffs <- numeric(n_permutations)
all_ts <- c(high_usage, low_usage)
n_high <- length(high_usage)

for (i in 1:n_permutations) {
  shuffled <- sample(all_ts)
  perm_high <- shuffled[1:n_high]
  perm_low <- shuffled[(n_high + 1):length(all_ts)]
  perm_diffs[i] <- mean(perm_high) - mean(perm_low)
}

# Calculate P-value (two-tailed)
p_value <- mean(abs(perm_diffs) >= abs(obs_diff))
print(paste("Permutation P-value:", p_value))

# Visualize the distribution
hist(perm_diffs, main="Distribution of Permuted Differences", 
     xlab="Difference in Mean TS%", col="skyblue")
abline(v = obs_diff, col="red", lwd=2)
```

Discrete Distribution (geometric)

modeling number of attempts to first score using TS% as the probability of success $p$
